preImport.title: Before you import
preImport.description: Choose the starting point that best matches your data. You can still adjust the configuration after automatic analysis.

overview.title: One occurrences CSV is enough to start.
overview.description: Import your records first. You can add sites, plots, spatial layers, or more specific values later.

read.title: 1. Read your columns
read.description: Niamoto inspects headers, identifiers, and geometries.

derive.title: 2. Derive taxonomy
derive.description: If family, genus, species, or id_taxonref are present, taxonomy can be extracted.

review.title: 3. Review before import
review.description: You keep control over file roles and detected links.

files.title: Choose the model that matches your data
files.description: Templates are only starting points: replace the example rows with your own data.

occurrences.title: Occurrences
occurrences.description: The main file: observations, taxa, and optionally coordinates.

sites.title: Sites / plots
sites.description: A complementary file describing sites or plots linked to occurrences.

checks.title: A few checks prevent the most common errors
checks.description: These points improve automatic detection without forcing you to configure import manually.

headers.title: Headers
headers.description: One clear header row with stable column names.

identifiers.title: Identifiers
identifiers.description: One unique identifier for occurrences, sites, or taxa that need links.

matchingValues.title: Matching values
matchingValues.description: Related files should reuse the same identifier values.

spatial.title: Spatial formats
spatial.description: GeoPackage or GeoJSON is more reliable than shapefile for detection.

advanced.title: Open these details only if this sounds like your case
advanced.description: These formats are useful, but more constrained than the standard occurrences path.

hierarchy.title: Hierarchies
hierarchy.description: For nested levels such as family > genus > species or country > region > plot.

classObject.title: Values already calculated by class
classObject.description: For attaching one entity to one measured object, one class, and one numeric value.

resources.title: Resources
resources.description: Download a template or check accepted formats without leaving this help.

start.title: You can start simply
start.description: One occurrences file is often enough to begin. If it also contains family, genus, species, or id_taxonref, Niamoto can derive the taxonomy from it.

occurrences.title: I have an occurrences file
occurrences.description: Observations, taxa, and optionally coordinates. This is the main file in the standard workflow.

siteReference.title: I also have sites / plots
siteReference.description: A list of sites, plots, or sampling locations to link with occurrences for spatial grouping.

advanced.title: I have a more specific case
advanced.description: Separate hierarchies, values already calculated by class, or stricter file formats.
advanced.hint: Open the panels below only if this sounds like your situation.

csv.title: CSV tables
csv.description: Delimited tables for datasets, references, metadata, and supporting values.

vector.title: Vector layers
vector.description: Spatial features with geometry, usually plots, areas, or boundaries.

raster.title: Raster layers
raster.description: GeoTIFF layers used as gridded environmental or spatial inputs.

preImport.shapefileNote: For shapefiles, prefer GeoPackage when possible. ZIP shapefile uploads are accepted, but automatic detection is most reliable with GeoPackage or GeoJSON.

items.headers: Keep one clear header row in CSV files.
items.identifiers: Use explicit identifier columns such as id, plot_id, or taxon_id.
items.matchingValues: Reuse the same identifier values between related files.
items.spatial: Prefer GeoPackage or GeoJSON for spatial data.

hierarchy.title: Do your data have nested levels?
hierarchy.summary: Use this for taxonomies or geographic levels such as family > genus > species or country > region > locality > plot.
hierarchy.meaningDescription: A hierarchy is a set of columns where each row moves from a broad level to a more precise level.
hierarchy.standardTaxonomyTitle: Standard case: taxonomy derived from occurrences
hierarchy.standardTaxonomyDescription: You usually do not need a separate taxonomy file. Put taxonomy columns directly in the occurrences CSV, for example id_taxonref, taxaname, family, genus, species, and infra.
hierarchy.separateColumnsDescription: Put at least two levels in the same CSV, each in its own column.
hierarchy.clearNamesDescription: Use names such as family, genus, species, country, region, locality, or plot.
hierarchy.orderDescription: Keep values consistent so broader levels contain fewer unique values than deeper levels.
hierarchy.identifierDescription: When possible, add a stable id such as taxon_id, plot_id, id_taxon, or id.

templates.title: Need a starting file?
templates.description: Download a small CSV model, then replace the example rows with your own data.

classObject.title: Do you have values already calculated by class?
classObject.summary: Use this only when one entity has numeric values split into named classes. Niamoto stores that as a class_object CSV.
classObject.meaningDescription: Each row links one entity to one measured object, an optional class, and one numeric value.
classObject.example: Example: plot_001 + forest_cover + forest + 8.4 means plot_001 has 8.4 units of forest cover in the forest class.
classObject.identifierDescription: Include one identifier column named entity_id, plot_id, shape_id, taxon_id, or id so each row can be attached to an entity.
classObject.numericValuesDescription: The class_value column must contain numeric values. Leave class_name empty only when the value is not split by class.

reference.deleteReferenceDescription: This action will remove the reference from configuration.
reference.deleteWarningHierarchical: Warning: linked datasets may stop working.
reference.noLevelsDefined: No levels defined. Click "Add" to define levels.

summary.emptyTable: This imported table is empty. Review the import configuration before using it downstream.

enrichment.title: API enrichment
enrichment.notConfiguredDescription: Configure external APIs before enriching this reference.
enrichment.failedDescription: The latest enrichment job needs attention.
enrichment.readyDescription: Sources are configured and ready to run.

spatialMap.title: Map
spatialMap.notMappableDescription: This reference does not expose a geometry column that can be mapped.
spatialMap.empty: No geometry is available for display.
spatialMap.layerOverviewDescription: Select a layer to load the interactive map.

hierarchy.noMetadataDescription: This reference is marked as hierarchical, but the imported table does not expose recognizable hierarchy columns.
hierarchy.hasMore: Only the first results are shown. Refine the search or expand a narrower branch.

form.linkToEntities: Column linking data to "{{reference}}" group entities
form.defineSchemaHint: Define schema fields to document data structure.

validation.outlierDescription: An outlier is a value that deviates significantly from other observations. These values may indicate data entry errors, interesting exceptional cases, or data issues.

iqr.name: IQR (Interquartile Range)
iqr.description: Robust method based on quartiles. Identifies values outside the 'normal' zone defined by the interquartile range.
iqr.details: Q1 = 25th percentile, Q3 = 75th percentile, IQR = Q3 - Q1. A value is considered an outlier if < Q1 - 1.5×IQR or > Q3 + 1.5×IQR.
iqr.bestFor: Asymmetric data or non-normal distributions. Standard in exploratory statistics.

zscore.name: Z-Score (Standard deviation)
zscore.description: Measures how many standard deviations a value is from the mean. Assumes normal distribution.
zscore.details: Z = (value - mean) / std deviation. A value with |Z| > 3 is considered an outlier (> 3 standard deviations from mean).
zscore.bestFor: Data following a normal (Gaussian) distribution. Physical measurements, heights, weights.
zscore.sensitivity: Low - only detects very extreme values

percentile.name: Percentile (1% - 99%)
percentile.description: Simply identifies the lowest 1% and highest 1% of values.
percentile.details: Any value below the 1st percentile or above the 99th percentile is an outlier. Simple and intuitive.
percentile.bestFor: When you want to identify a fixed percentage of extreme values, regardless of distribution.
percentile.sensitivity: Fixed - always detects exactly 2% of data (if present)

validation.valuesOutOfBounds: Values out of bounds according to {{method}} method

thresholdHints.percentile: 5% = excludes 5% extremes on each side (10% total)

geoCoverage.spatialAnalysisDescription: Run a spatial analysis to check the coverage of occurrences by imported shapes (uses geometries, may take time).

standards.title: Standard profiles
standards.description: Configure publication profiles separately from simple JSON API exports.
standards.empty: No standard profile is configured yet.
standards.profileOverviewHelp: This profile is saved. Edit it only when mappings, source, or outputs need to change.
standards.profileEditorHelp: Mappings and outputs belong to the standard profile, not to the simple collection API.
standards.mappingHelp: Map each standard term to a source field, static value, or generator.
standards.mappingReferenceHelp: Darwin Core Occurrence must map occurrence-grain data. Humboldt/Event should map event or inventory terms.
standards.autoConfigureHelp: Build a draft standard profile from imported fields.
standards.autoConfigured: Draft updated: {{count}} standard term(s) proposed from {{columns}} inspected source column(s). Review before saving.
standards.legacyHintDescription: Existing export "{{exportName}}" looks like {{standard}} output. Create a standard profile when you are ready to migrate it.
standards.compatibilityConfidenceHelp: Confidence {{confidence}}%: certainty level calculated from the evidence below. It is not a warning or a conformance score.

compatibilityExplanation.exactSourceGrain: No compatibility issue detected: the source is already at {{grain}} grain.
compatibilityExplanation.occurrenceRelation: Not 100%: compatibility relies on a detected relation to {{dataset}} ({{foreignKey}} -> {{targetField}}), not on a source directly at {{target}} grain.
compatibilityExplanation.plausible: Not 100%: the source is at {{source}} grain, while the profile targets {{target}} grain. A more precise source or relation is needed.
compatibilityExplanation.blocked: The source does not provide the grain expected by this standard.
compatibilityExplanation.inferred: Not 100%: compatibility relies on inferred evidence.

standards.publicationBlocked: Publication files are disabled until critical validation issues are resolved. API JSON can still be generated.
standards.noOutputs: No output is configured for this profile.
standards.draftRetention: Test artifacts are written under {{location}} and can be cleaned up manually.
standards.outputJsonPreviewFailed: Could not load the JSON preview.
